notes: OpenTable Hanoi neighborhood page (/neighborhood/vn/vietnam/hanoi-restaurants) repeatedly timed out via WebFetch. The 4 entries below were extracted from OpenTable's r/ search-result URLs via WebSearch snippets. Hanoi's OpenTable inventory is small relative to HCMC — most upscale Hanoi venues use phone / Capichi / PasGo rather than OpenTable.

restaurants.raw_address: InterContinental Hanoi Landmark72, Cau Giay Urban Area, Hanoi
restaurants.raw_notes: Inside InterContinental Hanoi Landmark72. Asia's Luxury Steakhouse by World Luxury Restaurant Awards. Wine Spectator Award of Excellence. Only steakhouse in Hanoi dry-aging beef in-house. 4.6 stars on OpenTable. Chef de Cuisine Tim Nolte.

restaurants.raw_address: InterContinental Hanoi Landmark72, Cau Giay Urban Area, Hanoi
restaurants.raw_notes: Inside InterContinental Hanoi Landmark72. Eclectic luxury dining with multiple cuisines and open cooking stations, panoramic Hanoi views. 4.8 stars on OpenTable. Best for breakfast / weekend brunch with Hanoi skyline.

restaurants.raw_address: Hoan Kiem, Ha Noi (near St. Joseph's Cathedral)
restaurants.raw_notes: Fine dining overlooking St. Joseph's Cathedral. Indochina + Gothic design. Contemporary Vietnamese setting suitable for special occasions.

restaurants.raw_address: Old Quarter, Hoàn Kiếm, Hanoi (~5 min from St. Joseph's Cathedral)
restaurants.raw_notes: Original founded 1994. Vietnamese culinary tradition redefined with Western flair. Tripadvisor top-rated. Per tabispavn the per-person spend is ~1000 JPY at lunch which would put it BELOW the 800k VND threshold for this list — but OpenTable lists it as $$ and dinner pricing trends higher. Borderline include; verify dinner pricing in enrichment.
